title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer and B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. The GTM Engineer will replace manual workflows and patchwork SaaS tools with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Responsibilities include building lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly. Ideal for someone who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team highly effective during a high-growth scaling phase.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM Engineer title, AI-native workflows, and RevOps automation expertise. Slight concern: role emphasizes coding/shipping internal tools—if Omri's strength is process design over engineering implementation, fit drops to 6-7.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Conveo and thought of you immediately—you're building the exact GTM infrastructure that RevOps leaders like Omri have been hacking together with 5 different tools. Your pipeline forecasting and Salesforce automation background would translate directly into their lead scoring models and AI agent workflows. They're scaling fast (Unilever, Google, Orange as customers) and need someone who can think operationally *and* execute technically.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Snorkel AI seeks a GTM Engineer to sit at the intersection of business systems, data, automation, and AI. You'll design and build workflows that help Sales, Marketing, Delivery, and Revenue Operations move faster, owning automations across the GTM stack using Salesforce, Gong, Apollo, Claude, Zapier, Make, and n8n. Requires 4+ years in GTM Engineering, Business Systems, RevOps, or Data Engineering, strong hands-on Salesforce experience, and workflow automation skills. A hands-on builder role for someone who enjoys solving messy operational problems with scalable systems and pragmatic engineering in a B2B SaaS environment.
score_reason: Perfect alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM background, Salesforce expertise, and passion for automation-driven operational scaling. The AI-native GTM focus and workflow engineering across modern stacks (Gong, Apollo, n8n) directly match his strengths, though this is slightly more hands-on technical than typical Head of RevOps roles.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building scalable GTM systems in B2B SaaS, and your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because it's exactly where I see the next revenue unlock happening: at the intersection of Salesforce, automation, and AI-powered workflows. I've owned similar operational transformations (CAC/LTV optimization, pipeline forecasting, multi-system integrations), and the idea of designing workflows that actually help Sales and RevOps move faster rather than creating more busywork is refreshingly pragmatic. I'd love to explore how my background in building messy operational systems could contribute to Snorkel's GTM velocity.
